findings.summary: Docstring bloat on trivial wrapper functions in logging_utils.py
evidence: log_substep has a 10-line docstring for a 2-line function that calls logger.info with an f-string
log_stage has a 12-line docstring (with example output) for a 4-line function
log_success has a 1-line docstring but still has redundant emoji-selection logic that restates the function name
findings.suggestion: Reduce docstrings on trivial formatting helpers to a single-line description; remove example-output blocks from functions whose behavior is fully described by their name and signature.

findings.summary: Three thin module-level pass-through functions in health_check.py wrap a singleton with no added policy
evidence: quick_health_check() calls _get_checker().is_healthy() — one statement, no logic
get_health_status() calls _get_checker().get_health_status() — one statement, no logic
get_health_metrics() calls _get_checker().get_metrics() — one statement, no logic
All three are purely forwarding the call; callers could work directly with _get_checker() or a cached public singleton
findings.suggestion: Expose a module-level singleton directly (e.g. _checker exported as health_checker) or collapse into a single get_health() entry point that returns the full status object, eliminating the three one-liner wrappers.

findings.summary: generate_pipeline_report unpacks ReportExtras field-by-field into locals that are immediately re-used only once
evidence: Lines 68-73 unpack all six fields of extras into identically-named locals (test_results, validation_results, performance_metrics, error_summary, output_statistics, project_name)
Each local is then used exactly once when constructing PipelineReport — the indirection adds no clarity and reads as AI padding
The pattern is: `x = extras.x if extras is not None else None` repeated six times
findings.suggestion: Use a dataclass helper or direct attribute access pattern: `r = extras or ReportExtras()` then reference `r.field` directly in the PipelineReport constructor call.

findings.summary: logging_utils.py is a re-export facade that adds no policy — callers import through it but logic lives in four split modules
evidence: logging_utils imports from logging_helpers, logging_progress, logging_formatters, and logging_constants, then re-exports everything in __all__
The split modules are not independently documented as stable boundaries — they are internal implementation details surfaced through the facade
Callers that import format_duration, calculate_eta, JSONFormatter, EMOJIS from logging_utils are traversing an extra indirection hop with zero benefit; the split was done for file size, not ownership
findings.suggestion: Either collapse the internal split modules back into logging_utils (preferred for a module this cohesive) or promote them to explicit stable public packages with their own __all__ and let callers import directly, removing logging_utils as a re-export layer.

findings.summary: ReportExtras is a single-use parameter bag that duplicates PipelineReport's optional fields
evidence: ReportExtras has six fields (test_results, validation_results, performance_metrics, error_summary, output_statistics, project_name) that mirror the optional fields of PipelineReport exactly
Its sole purpose is to bundle the six kwargs of generate_pipeline_report — it is never stored, mutated, or reused across call sites

The docstring describes it as grouping six optional fields 'so callers only construct what they need' — this is parameter-bag overengineering for a function that could simply use **kwargs or Optional keyword args
findings.suggestion: Remove ReportExtras and accept the optional fields directly as keyword arguments on generate_pipeline_report, or make them part of PipelineReport's constructor directly. The indirection class provides no cohesion benefit.
